Metadata.title: JWeb | Esdras Portfolio
Metadata.description: Institutional website and native iOS app for JWeb, a digital agency. Next.js 16, React 19, TypeScript, multilingual (PT/EN/ES), with an APNs push-notification platform (PostgreSQL + Drizzle) and a Capacitor iOS app.
Metadata.keywords: digital agency, digital solutions, Next.js, React, TypeScript, internationalization, CMS, iOS app, Capacitor, push notifications, APNs, PostgreSQL, Drizzle
Metadata.creator: Esdras

JWeb.title: COMPLETE INSTITUTIONAL WEBSITE AND iOS APP FOR DIGITAL AGENCY
JWeb.description: Institutional website and native iOS app for JWeb, a digital agency. Built with Next.js 16, React 19 and TypeScript, fully localized in Portuguese, English and Spanish, and scoring 90+ on Lighthouse. Content runs through a Keystatic headless CMS with a Markdoc blog; forms are validated with React Hook Form, Zod and reCAPTCHA v3, and transactional email is sent with Resend. Analytics run on Google Tag Manager behind GDPR Consent Mode v2. The project also includes a push-notification platform: a PostgreSQL database with Drizzle ORM for device tokens, an admin console to compose and broadcast campaigns, and an APNs sender that delivers in batches with Redis rate limiting and automatic pruning of invalid tokens. The companion iOS app is built with Capacitor, registering push tokens to the backend and supporting universal links and App Tracking Transparency. Styled with Tailwind CSS 4 and accessible shadcn/ui components.

Metadata.description: A cross-platform desktop app that turns screenshots into solved code problems. Built for developers and students prepping for coding interviews.
Metadata.keywords: ai coding, screenshot to solution, electron, nestjs, invisible coding app

PhantomCode.title: A cross-platform desktop app that turns screenshots into solved code problems
PhantomCode.description: Phantom Code is a desktop app built with Electron and React, backed by a NestJS API. It captures code from screenshots, extracts it with AI and returns working solutions for programming problems.

Metadata.title: Tarevity | Esdras Portfolio
Metadata.description: Tarevity is a task management app built with Next.js 16, TypeScript, Prisma ORM and PostgreSQL. Fast, focused and built to keep your daily to-do list out of your way.
Metadata.keywords: task management, to-do app, Next.js, TypeScript, Prisma, PostgreSQL, Better Auth, Redis
Metadata.creator: Esdras Santos

Tarevity.title: a modern task management app
Tarevity.description: Tarevity is a task management app built with Next.js 16, TypeScript, Prisma ORM and PostgreSQL. Fast, focused and built to keep your daily to-do list out of your way.

Metadata.title: Nexebra Trail | Esdras Portfolio
Metadata.description: Official website for Trilhos da Nexebra Trail, a trail running event promoting the sport and the region's natural heritage.
Metadata.keywords: Nexebra Trail, trail running, sports event, Next.js, Tailwind CSS, TypeScript, Portugal
Metadata.creator: Esdras Santos

NexebraTrail.title: Sports Events and Trail Running
NexebraTrail.description: Website for the Trilhos da Nexebra Trail - TNT® event, created to promote sports and natural heritage. The event has already welcomed over 4,500 athletes. The site was developed with a focus on performance and seamless user experience, utilizing Next.js, Tailwind CSS, Shadcn UI, and TypeScript.

Metadata.title: PCByte | Esdras Portfolio
Metadata.description: PCByte is a full-stack technology e-commerce platform with a Medusa.js backend, Next.js storefront, Expo mobile app, and Strapi CMS. Includes custom modules for S3, PayPal, bank transfer, Meilisearch, Odoo and Resend, with security-first practices across the stack.
Metadata.keywords: e-commerce, Medusa, Next.js, Strapi, React Native, Expo, Meilisearch, PayPal, Odoo, Redis, AWS S3, headless commerce
Metadata.creator: Esdras Santos

PCByte.title: Full-stack headless e-commerce for a technology store
PCByte.description: PCByte is a complete e-commerce ecosystem for a technology store, architected across four independent projects. The backend is built on Medusa.js with Redis caching and custom modules for AWS S3 dual-bucket storage, PayPal payments, manual bank transfer, Meilisearch indexing, Odoo ERP synchronization, transactional emails via Resend, weight-based fulfillment, product attributes, media, and reviews. The Next.js 16 storefront uses React 19, next-intl, TanStack Query, Zustand, Jotai and ships with Redis-backed rate limiting, Google reCAPTCHA v3, honeypot fields, DOMPurify sanitization and a Strapi-powered headless CMS. The mobile app is built with Expo and React Native, using secure storage, biometric authentication and MMKV. Strapi (PostgreSQL + S3) powers editorial content. Quality is enforced through unit, integration and Playwright end-to-end tests on the backend and storefront, plus a Vitest suite on the frontend and Jest unit tests on mobile.

Metadata.title: Zipway | Esdras Portfolio
Metadata.description: Zipway is a fast and minimal URL shortener with a Next.js frontend and a high-performance Go backend, designed for low-latency redirects and clean link analytics.
Metadata.keywords: URL shortener, link shortener, Next.js, Go, Golang, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, Redis
Metadata.creator: Esdras Santos

Zipway.title: A fast URL shortener powered by Go and Next.js
Zipway.description: Zipway is a modern URL shortener focused on speed, simplicity and reliability. The frontend is built with Next.js, TypeScript and Tailwind CSS, delivering a clean and responsive interface for generating and managing short links. The backend is written in Go for maximum performance and low-latency redirects, exposing a lightweight HTTP API. The system is designed to scale horizontally and serves the live shortener at shly.pt.
